remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Not applicable — sepsis is an acute life-threatening syndrome; hospital mortality 15-25% for sepsis, 30-45% for septic shock with standard care; long-term complications (post-sepsis syndrome) in 50-70% of survivors
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Hour-1 Bundle (blood cultures + antibiotics + fluids + vasopressors): 30-40% mortality reduction vs delayed care; vasopressin as vasopressor adjunct reduces vasopressor requirements; corticosteroids in septic shock: 3-5% absolute mortality reduction (ADRENAL, APROCCHSS); no truly curative intervention beyond source control
remission.gap_size: Large — compliance with Hour-1 Bundle varies enormously across hospitals; 3-6 hour bundle completion only in 50-70% of hospitals
remission.barrier_detail: Sepsis recognition delay is the primary driver of mortality; time-to-antibiotics is the single best predictor of outcome (each hour delay increases mortality 4-7%); early goal-directed therapy (EGDT) as originally described was abandoned after ProCESS/ARISE/ProMISe trials showed no benefit of specific protocol over usual care, creating confusion about what 'appropriate' standard care is
remission.last_soc_change: 2021 — Surviving Sepsis Campaign updated Hour-1 Bundle; 2020 lactate monitoring formalised; steroid guidance revised after ADRENAL and APROCCHSS; Drotrecogin alfa (Xigris) withdrawn 2011 after Phase 3 failure; treatment approach largely unchanged since surviving sepsis campaign 2004
remission.outdated_treatments: Drotrecogin alfa (Xigris — withdrawn 2011); tight glycaemic control (110-140 target) — NICE-SUGAR showed harm, now permissive glycaemia); early goal-directed therapy as originally specified (CVP/ScvO2 protocol — abandoned after 2015 trials); routine fresh frozen plasma prophylactically without bleeding indication
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Thiamine supplementation (benefit in metabolic acidosis subgroup); vitamin C + hydrocortisone (LOVIT trial vitamin C alone null, CITRIS-ALI null; combination remains studied); IVIG (limited evidence, meta-analysis equivocal); IL-6 receptor antagonists for cytokine storm sepsis subphenotype
remission.frontier_signal: Host-response phenotyping (SRS1 vs SRS2 — hyperinflammatory vs hyper-immunosuppressed — allows phenotype-targeted treatment; VENOMS trial); IL-7 for immunoparalysis subtype; GM-CSF for monocyte restoration; AI-based early sepsis prediction (Epic Sepsis Model — widely implemented, performance debated); bacteriophage therapy for MDR infections
